children.heading: Eligible employees
children.label: a Eligible employees
children.text: An employee who elects to receive a separation allowance under an employee protection agreement entered into or a benefit schedule prescribed under may, if so provided under such agreement or benefit schedule, receive from the Board reasonable expenses for training in qualified institutions for new career opportunities.section 1005 of this title
must first exhaust any Federal educational benefits available to such employee under any existing program; and
must begin his course of training within 2 years following the date of such employee’s separation from employment with the Rock Island Railroad.

children.heading: Determination of reasonable expenses by Board
children.label: c Determination of reasonable expenses by Board
children.text: Reasonable expenses for assistance under this section shall be determined by the Board on the basis of an application therefor filed by an employee with the Board.

children.heading: Assistance prohibited afterApril 1, 1984
children.label: d Assistance prohibited afterApril 1, 1984
children.text: No assistance may be provided under this section after .April 1, 1984
the term “expenses” means actual, reasonable expenses paid for room, board, tuition, fees, or educational material in an amount not to exceed $3,000; and

the term “qualified institution” means an educational institution accredited for payment by the Veterans’ Administration under chapter 36 of title 38, or a State-accredited institution which has been in existence for not less than 2 years.
